skillName: Position decimals on a number line (incl midpoint using a calculator)
misconceptionSummary: Students think the midpoint is just the smaller number plus one — they don't see it as equidistant.

visuals.ruleText: Every decimal has an exact home between two integers on the number line
visuals.subText: 0.7 lives seven tenths of the way from 0 to 1

steps.narration: Decimals do not jump between integers — they fill in every gap. Zero point seven is exactly seven tenths of the way from zero to one. Its position is precise, not approximate.

lines.text: Midpoint = equally far from both ends
Distance from A to mid = distance from mid to B
This is why we use (A + B) ÷ 2

steps.narration: The midpoint is the one value that is equally far from both endpoints. Adding both values together and halving the total always lands you in the exact middle.
One point five sits exactly zero point three from each endpoint. Equal distances — that confirms it is the true midpoint.
One point five is the midpoint. The sum divided by two always gives the value that is exactly halfway.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: guessing the midpoint by eye or adding 1 to the smaller number. Use (A + B) ÷ 2 — it guarantees equal distance from both endpoints.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The midpoint is equidistant from both ends. Add the two values, divide by two — you can't guess your way to accuracy.
